final_text:
We need the standard enthalpy change of combustion of H₂.  
The combustion reaction is:  
\[
2H_2(g) + O_2(g) \rightarrow 2H_2O(l)
\]  
The standard enthalpy change for this reaction is:  
\[
\Delta H^\circ = 2 \times \Delta H_f^\circ(H_2O(l)) - [2 \times \Delta H_f^\circ(H_2(g)) + \Delta H_f^\circ(O_2(g))]
\]  
Since \(\Delta H_f^\circ\) for elements in their standard state is zero:  
\[
\Delta H^\circ = 2 \times (-285.8) = -571.6 \ \text{kJ}
\]  
This is for 2 moles of H₂, so per mole:  
\[
\frac{-571.6}{2} = -285.8 \ \text{kJ mol}^{-1}
\]  

[ANSWER]-285.8[/ANSWER]
